intro: Salpiglossis lomana, also known as Painted Tongue or Lomandra, is a flowering plant native to South America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Salpiglossis lomana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
